persona.name: Alex Morgan
persona.title: Business Development Representative

demographics.education: Bachelor's in Business Administration or Communications; Minor in Data Science or IT preferred
demographics.location: Urban Tech Hubs (US: SF, NYC, Austin; UK: London; EMEA: Berlin)

motivations: Hitting and exceeding monthly quota (Source: Inbound/Outbound)
Career progression into Account Executive or Sales Engineer role
Being seen as a 'trusted advisor' rather than a 'pushy salesperson'
Solving complex operational problems for C-suite buyers
pain_points: Long sales cycles (6-12 months) due to AI procurement/compliance reviews
Gatekeepers who don't understand the technical value of AI vs. traditional software
Competing with hyperscalers (AWS/Azure/Google) who bundle AI for 'free'
Overcoming the 'Black Box' fear—proving explainable AI (XAI) to non-technical stakeholders
information_sources: Internal Product Demos and Engineering Changelogs

typical_day: 08:00 - 09:00: Review LinkedIn feed for trigger events (funding rounds, new CTO hires)
09:00 - 10:00: Account research & building 5 prioritized target lists
12:00 - 13:00: Lunch + listening to a sales podcast
13:00 - 15:00: Personalized video prospecting (Loom or Sendspark) & Email follow-ups
15:00 - 17:00: Qualifying inbound leads (BANT framework) and handoff to AEs

common_objections: We don't have the data quality for AI.
It's too expensive compared to our current legacy system.
We are worried about data privacy/IP leakage.
We already have an internal data science team.
objection_handling.response_tactics: Focus on ROI calculators; highlight vector database security; pivot to augmenting internal teams, not replacing them.

buying_triggers: Competitor just implemented an AI feature
New data privacy regulation passed (e.g., EU AI Act)
Company just raised Series B/C funding
Internal data breach or security audit failure

email_subject_lines: Is your data ready for GenAI?
preferred_engagement.social_platform: LinkedIn (posts about AI trends, not sales pitches)
